name: Harbor Notes AgentSite
description: A calm launch page for Harbor Notes, a tiny field-journal workflow for solo operators who need decisions, receipts, and next actions in one durable place.
heroHeadline: Harbor Notes turns messy calls into durable next actions
heroLede: A calm proof-of-concept for consultants and fractional operators who need decisions, receipts, and owners captured without a heavy project-management rollout.
brief: Create a restrained, premium static landing page for Harbor Notes: a disposable but polished proof-of-concept for independent consultants and fractional operators who capture messy meeting notes, extract decisions, and hand off a clear next-action log. The site should feel specific, trustworthy, and operational rather than hype-driven.
audience: Independent consultants who leave meetings with scattered action items
Fractional operators maintaining client-facing decision logs
Solo founders testing whether a lightweight notes-to-next-actions workflow deserves a real product
visualDirection: quiet harbor-at-night palette, editorial spacing, compact proof cards, restrained premium SaaS without fake logos or metrics

sections.title: From meeting residue to a reliable action log
sections.body: Harbor Notes is framed around the moment after a client call: rough notes, uncertain owners, and decisions that need to become a durable handoff without turning into a full project-management rollout.

sections.title: Made for operators who sell clarity
sections.body: The page speaks to consultants and fractional leaders who need a credible way to show that decisions were captured, next steps were named, and the source material remains available for review.

sections.title: Proof is the workflow, not inflated outcomes
sections.body: The site should point to observable artifacts: a brief, a contract, QA commands, and a deployable static repository. It should not invent customers, revenue impact, adoption counts, or guaranteed productivity gains.

proofArtifacts.label: Decision log artifact
proofArtifacts.body: A structured page section names what changed, who owns it, and what needs review next.

proofArtifacts.label: Repo-local guardrails
proofArtifacts.body: Generated contracts define allowed claims, forbidden claims, approval boundaries, and no-payment mode.

proofArtifacts.label: QA before handoff
proofArtifacts.body: The generated project includes npm run qa for contracts, claims, links, SEO basics, and Astro build verification.

allowedClaims: Payment remains disabled in no-payment mode
The page is a disposable proof-of-concept, not a production service promise
approvalRequired: Adding analytics, cookies, tracking pixels, or third-party forms
Adding payment links or enabling payment mode
Claiming customer adoption, revenue impact, or quantified productivity gains
Changing deployment target away from GitHub Pages
Introducing server-side runtime or a database
